qa.overall_assessment: The four corrections from iter1 are substantially accurate: M5 removal is correct (both files committed), AC17-AC19 are confirmed committed and passing, the manifest is confirmed to contain only AC1-AC16, and the test count is correctly stated as 30 throughout the ticket and context JSON strategy. However, two new blocking gaps prevent dispatch: (1) context-20260524-101700-A.json requirement.clarified still says 'add 3 new integration-level ACs ... and commit the two untracked files' — the stale iter1 instruction — which will misdirect dev; (2) docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-A.json was not updated and still carries only the original 5 ACs from 10:17 UTC; AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT are absent from the machine-readable AC file that QA Step 11 Phase 5 checks.

correction_audit._doc: Tracks whether each of the four iter1 objections is correctly addressed in the revised BA spec.

objection_1_m5_stale_untracked.iter1_finding: M5 requirement 'commit both untracked files' was stale — files already committed in becffc6.
objection_1_m5_stale_untracked.correction_claimed: M5 removed from Must Have; note added in MoSCoW section and ticket Goal/Context fields.
objection_1_m5_stale_untracked.evidence: Ticket line 101: 'Removed from prior spec: M5 (Commit both untracked files) — both files are already committed (commit becffc6, confirmed by git ls-files in this BA session). No git add/commit action needed for those files.' MoSCoW Must Have section has only M1-M4 with no M5. git ls-files confirmed both files tracked.

objection_2_ac17_ac19_already_exist.iter1_finding: BA claimed prior directory 'does NOT include quoted-arg cases'; AC17-19 already existed and passed.
objection_2_ac17_ac19_already_exist.correction_claimed: Scope section updated; M3 revised to 'register AC17-19 in manifest' rather than 'create new files'; W4 added explicitly forbidding new test files under tests/generated/20260524-101700-A/.
objection_2_ac17_ac19_already_exist.evidence: Ticket line 55: 'Correction from prior spec (Objection 2 addressed)' — explicitly acknowledges AC17-19 existed. M3 now reads 'Add AC17, AC18, and AC19 to tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[]'. W4 reads 'New test files under tests/generated/20260524-101700-A/ — AC17/AC18/AC19 in the existing directory cover the three quoted-arg cases.' QA independently ran pytest on all three: 3 passed in 0.82s. git ls-files confirms all three committed in 20a45af.

objection_3_manifest_gap_not_mentioned.iter1_finding: Manifest tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[] missing AC17-19; BA spec had no mention of this gap.
objection_3_manifest_gap_not_mentioned.correction_claimed: AC-MANIFEST criterion added; M3 explicitly targets manifest registration; Technical Hints section specifies exact JSON entries to append; Risk 4 added for append-only constraint.
objection_3_manifest_gap_not_mentioned.evidence: Ticket line 150-154: AC-MANIFEST criterion with GIVEN/WHEN/THEN. M3 at line 98 targets manifest registration. Lines 182-221 give exact JSON schema for three new manifest entries. Manifest file read programmatically: schema_version 1.1, active_tests count 16, AC17/AC18/AC19 absent (confirmed). Context JSON success_criteria includes 'AC-MANIFEST: tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[] contains 19 entries (AC1-AC19)'.

objection_4_unit_test_count_drift.iter1_finding: Context JSON development_approach.strategy stated '26 unit tests'; actual count is 30.
objection_4_unit_test_count_drift.correction_claimed: Strategy updated to '30 passed (not 26; TestDollarVariableRegression class adds 3 tests)'; M2 updated to '30 unit tests total'; AC-UNIT updated to 'output contains 30 passed'.
objection_4_unit_test_count_drift.evidence: Context JSON strategy field: 'confirm 30 passed (not 26; TestDollarVariableRegression class adds 3 tests)'. Ticket M2: '30 unit tests total'. Ticket AC-UNIT: 'output contains 30 passed (not 26)'. QA ran pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py: 30 passed in 0.03s. Count is accurate.

ba_validation_dimensions.detail:
All factual evidence claims in the corrected spec are independently verifiable and confirmed:

1. Both source files committed: git ls-files hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py returns both paths. git log --oneline confirms commit becffc6 for both.

2. AC17/AC18/AC19 exist on disk and pass: ls tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/ shows all three files (mtime 10:27). git ls-files confirms all three tracked in commit 20a45af. pytest: 3 passed in 0.82s.

3. Manifest contains only AC1-AC16: programmatic read confirms schema_version 1.1, active_tests[] count = 16, AC17/AC18/AC19 absent.

4. Unit test count is 30: pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py reports '30 passed in 0.03s'. TestDollarVariableRegression class confirmed present with 3 test methods. TestDangerCommandArgPreservation present with 4 test methods at lines 213-239.

5. DANGER_COMMANDS branch at lines 264-273: read confirms 'if cmd_word in DANGER_COMMANDS:' at line 264, unquoting logic via text[1:-1] at line 270. DANGER_COMMANDS frozenset at line 24 confirmed.

6. BA's live Python execution claims are reproducible: strip_non_executable_contexts('killall "happy"') returns 'killall happy'; strip_non_executable_contexts('pkill -f "claude"') returns 'pkill -f claude'; strip_non_executable_contexts('kill "-9" 1234') returns 'kill -9 1234'.

findings.label: clarified_field_still_stale
findings.detail:
context-20260524-101700-A.json requirement.clarified still reads: 'The DANGER_COMMANDS branch in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py _process_segment() must unquote (expose content) rather than blank quoted arguments when the command word is in the danger set. The on-disk code already implements this correctly. Dev must verify correctness, add 3 new integration-level ACs (hook exit-code tests for killall "happy", pkill -f "claude", kill "-9" 1234), and commit the two untracked files.'

This is the iter1 instruction verbatim. 'Add 3 new integration-level ACs' contradicts W4 in the corrected ticket (which forbids creating new test files). 'Commit the two untracked files' contradicts the corrected codebase_state (both files are committed). Dev reading the clarified field will receive contradictory instructions relative to the MoSCoW Must Have requirements.

The clarified field is a primary dev-instruction-path field — BA agents write it as the concise 'what dev must do' summary. The discrepancy between this field and the corrected ticket content is material.
findings.recommendation: Update requirement.clarified in context-20260524-101700-A.json to: 'The DANGER_COMMANDS branch in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py _process_segment() is correctly implemented and committed (commit becffc6). Dev must: (1) confirm the branch is present at lines 264-273; (2) confirm pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py reports 30 passed; (3) append AC17, AC18, and AC19 to tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[]. No code changes. No new test file creation. No git commit of source files needed.'

ba_validation_dimensions.detail: BA's investigation for the corrected spec is complete. The prior-attempt failure analysis accurately records both failed attempts with correct failure categories. BA identified the manifest gap independently and produced exact JSON schema for the three new manifest entries. The DANGER_COMMANDS set, line references, and unquoting logic are all correctly documented and verified by live Python execution.

findings.label: acceptance_criteria_json_not_updated
findings.detail:
docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-A.json was NOT updated after the BA spec revision. It still carries only 5 ACs from the original 10:17 UTC version: AC-NEW-1, AC-NEW-2, AC-NEW-3, AC-REGRESSION-1, AC-REGRESSION-2. The timestamp is still '2026-05-24T10:17:00Z'. The two new ACs from the corrected spec — AC-MANIFEST (manifest registration verification) and AC-UNIT (30 unit tests assertion) — are absent.

This file is the machine-readable AC contract referenced by the context JSON field 'acceptance_criteria_path'. QA Step 11 Phase 5 verifies against this file. A dev verification system using this file will not enforce AC-MANIFEST or AC-UNIT.

File content confirmed by reading: 5 entries only. 'AC-MANIFEST' string not found via grep. 'AC-UNIT' string not found via grep. 'manifest' string not found via grep.
findings.recommendation: Update docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-A.json to include AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT entries with runnable check recipes. AC-MANIFEST check: parse tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json and assert len(active_tests) == 19 and all(['AC17','AC18','AC19'] subset of ac_ids). AC-UNIT check: subprocess pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py and assert '30 passed' in output. Timestamp must be updated to reflect the revision date.

ba_validation_dimensions.detail:
The unit test count drift from iter1 (26 vs 30) is corrected in all dev-instruction-path fields. Strategy now says '30 passed (not 26)'. M2 says '30 unit tests total'. AC-UNIT says 'output contains 30 passed (not 26 — TestDollarVariableRegression adds 3 tests)'. The acceptance criteria JSON AC check recipes (subprocess_stdin for hook exit codes) are unchanged and were verified correct by QA iter1. No new spec-text-vs-execution-drift issues found in the corrected ticket markdown or context JSON strategy.

Note: the acceptance_criteria_json_not_updated finding (dimension: affected_file_accuracy) is a different class of issue — it is a missing artifact rather than a recipe-vs-execution mismatch.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: bash_context_strip.py incorrectly strips quoted arguments from DANGER COMMANDS themselves (killall, pkill, kill, rm)
success_criteria_results.criterion: strip_non_executable_contexts('killall "happy"') returns a string containing 'happy'; hook exits 2 for killall "happy"
success_criteria_results.verification_method: git ls-files + pytest AC17-AC19 + code read
success_criteria_results.details: DANGER_COMMANDS branch confirmed at lines 264-273. test_AC17 passes (exit 2). strip_non_executable_contexts('killall "happy"') returns 'killall happy'.
success_criteria_results.evidence: git ls-files confirms committed. pytest: test_AC17 PASSED in 0.82s. Code read: line 264 'if cmd_word in DANGER_COMMANDS:', line 270 'out_d.append(text[1:-1])'.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: bypass is confirmed: killall "happy" exits 0 instead of 2
success_criteria_results.details: test_AC17 passes, which tests hook exit code = 2 for killall "happy".

success_criteria_results.criterion: Three new integration-level ACs each exit 2 and are registered in manifest active_tests[]
success_criteria_results.details: All three test files pass. Manifest registration is the sole dev deliverable (AC17-AC19 not yet in manifest). The tests themselves are committed and passing; the user requirement criterion 'three tests exist and exit 2' is satisfied by committed files. Manifest registration is required to fully satisfy R3's 'registered' clause but is correctly scoped as the sole dev task in M3.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest AC17/AC18/AC19: 3 passed in 0.82s. Manifest: active_tests count = 16, AC17/AC18/AC19 absent (confirmed programmatically).

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: do not remove the context_strip capability entirely
success_criteria_results.criterion: AC-REGRESSION-1: codex exec with killall+claude in prompt body still exits 0
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Code read of _process_segment() non-shell path + AC13 confirmed passing in prior QA iter2
success_criteria_results.details: Non-shell path at lines 275-289 unchanged; codex not in DANGER_COMMANDS; its quoted arguments are still blanked. AC13 confirmed passing in QA iter2. False-positive fix preserved.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Code read: lines 275-289 non-shell path intact. Prior QA iter2 report: AC13 exit 0 confirmed.

all_findings.issue: requirement.clarified still contains iter1 instructions: 'add 3 new integration-level ACs' and 'commit the two untracked files'. These contradict the corrected ticket's W4 (no new test files) and corrected codebase_state (files committed). Dev reading this field will receive misdirecting instructions.
all_findings.recommendation: Rewrite clarified field to describe the actual remaining dev task: confirm DANGER_COMMANDS branch, confirm 30/30 unit tests, append AC17-19 to manifest active_tests[]. Remove 'add 3 new integration-level ACs' and 'commit the two untracked files' from the clarified text.

all_findings.issue: Acceptance criteria JSON was not updated after BA spec revision. Still carries original 5 ACs from 10:17 UTC. AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT are absent. Timestamp still reads 10:17:00Z. The machine-readable AC contract does not reflect the corrected BA spec.
all_findings.recommendation: Regenerate or update acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-A.json to include AC-MANIFEST (check: parse manifest and assert 19 entries with AC17/AC18/AC19 present) and AC-UNIT (check: pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py output contains '30 passed'). Update timestamp to reflect revision time.

all_findings.issue: root_cause_commit says 'N/A — untracked files; the DANGER_COMMANDS fix is already present on disk but not committed'. timeline says 'On-disk code was updated (untracked) to add the DANGER_COMMANDS unquoting branch.' Both are historically accurate descriptions of the pre-commit state but are now factually stale. These are diagnostic/historical fields not in the dev instruction path, so they do not misdirect dev. The codebase_state field (the dev-instruction-path field) is correctly updated.
all_findings.recommendation: Non-blocking. Update root_cause_commit to reference becffc6 and update timeline to note the commit. Low priority — these fields are not read by dev's task execution path.

failures.issue: Stale iter1 instruction in clarified field: 'add 3 new integration-level ACs' and 'commit the two untracked files' contradict corrected ticket content.
failures.recommendation: Rewrite clarified field to reflect current task: manifest registration only.

failures.issue: Acceptance criteria JSON not updated. AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT absent from machine-readable contract. Timestamp stale at 10:17 UTC.
failures.recommendation: Update acceptance criteria JSON to include AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT with runnable check recipes.

summary.reject_reason: Two major gaps prevent dispatch: (1) context JSON requirement.clarified field retains iter1 instructions that contradict the corrected ticket — will misdirect dev; (2) acceptance-criteria JSON not updated — AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT absent from machine-readable contract. All four iter1 corrections are otherwise accurate and verified. The sole remaining dev deliverable (manifest registration of AC17-19) is correctly scoped. BA needs only to fix these two artifact inconsistencies before dispatch.

failed_criteria: scope_alignment: context JSON requirement.clarified still contains iter1 stale instructions ('add 3 new integration-level ACs', 'commit the two untracked files')
affected_file_accuracy: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-A.json not updated — AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT absent
major_issues: context-20260524-101700-A.json requirement.clarified must be rewritten to: 'Dev must: (1) confirm DANGER_COMMANDS branch at lines 264-273 is present and committed; (2) confirm pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py reports 30 passed; (3) append AC17, AC18, AC19 to tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[]. No code changes. No new test file creation. No source file commits needed.' Remove all references to 'add 3 new integration-level ACs' and 'commit the two untracked files'.

docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-A.json must be updated to include AC-MANIFEST entry (method: parse manifest, assert active_tests count == 19 and AC17/AC18/AC19 present) and AC-UNIT entry (method: subprocess pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py, assert '30 passed' in stdout). Timestamp must be updated to revision time (approximately 2026-05-24T12:30:00Z).
refined_context.recommended_approach: BA makes two targeted fixes to artifacts only — no ticket logic change needed. Fix 1: rewrite context JSON requirement.clarified to describe manifest-registration-only task. Fix 2: add AC-MANIFEST and AC-UNIT entries to acceptance-criteria JSON and update timestamp. All four prior iter1 corrections are verified correct and do not need re-examination. The corrected ticket markdown is accurate and complete.
refined_context.what_is_correct_in_ba: All four iter1 corrections verified accurate: (1) M5 removed — both files confirmed committed in becffc6; (2) M3 correctly revised to manifest registration — AC17-19 confirmed committed and passing in 20a45af; (3) AC-MANIFEST criterion correctly present in ticket markdown with 19-entry target; (4) test count correctly stated as 30 in strategy, M2, and AC-UNIT. The sole remaining dev deliverable (append AC17-19 to manifest active_tests[]) is accurately scoped and technically correct. The exact JSON entries provided in Technical Hints match the existing manifest schema exactly.
